text.title: Requirements Traceability — Agentic Workflow and Gotchas
text.summary: Four bounded subagent jobs, two-shot prompt patterns for link proposal and impact walk, and LLM failure modes specific to traceability.

rule.statement: Limit each subagent to one of four jobs; agents must never touch the matrix file directly — only source artifact frontmatter: (1) propose typed links with quoted evidence and confidence, (2) detect orphans (no parent or no child), (3) detect cycles and horizontal conflicts, (4) impact analysis on a change request.
rule.rationale: Mixing jobs produces runaway agents that bulk-modify the matrix; separating them enables human review of each pass's diff before regeneration.

example.note: Link-proposal prompt — five roles stated every call, requires quoted evidence from both artifacts.
Impact-analysis prompt — graph-only; no inference of artifacts outside supplied graph.
example.code: Given the link graph (JSON) and change request CR-{n}: List every artifact within 2 hops of the changed REQ. Group by type. Mark each Update / Review / No-op. Use ONLY the supplied graph; do not infer artifacts not present as nodes. Output: impact table (artifact_id, type, action, rationale) + one-line effort estimate.

antipattern.description: Hallucinated links — agent proposes a link but cannot quote evidence from both artifacts.
antipattern.reason: Require verbatim quote from source AND target; auto-reject confidence below 0.9; never accept a link the model proposes but cannot quote.

antipattern.description: Role conflation — agent confuses satisfies with verifies or derives.
antipattern.reason: Restate the full five-role vocabulary in the system prompt every turn; do not rely on the model retaining it across turns.

antipattern.description: Forward bias — agent readily generates BR → FR links and misses TC → REQ (backward) and FR ↔ FR (horizontal).
antipattern.reason: Run three separate passes, one per direction; combine results before human review.

antipattern.description: Bulk operation runaway — agent edits 500 links in one call with no diff review.
antipattern.reason: Cap any agent run at 20 link edits; require human review of the diff before the generator regenerates the matrix.

antipattern.description: Stale corpus during long agent runs — links point to artifacts that changed after run start.
antipattern.reason: Lock the source set with a git SHA at run start; reject the run if the SHA moves before merge.

list.item: Link confidence below 0.9 — human reviews before merge
Any conflicts edge proposed — human resolves before accepting
Coverage drop greater than 1 percentage point from last baseline
